question.text: Why does an American-style option typically command a higher premium than an otherwise identical European-style option?

answer.text: Because the American option provides the additional flexibility of early exercise at any time before expiry
Because American options are traded on larger exchanges with more liquidity
Because American options have lower margin requirements
Because American options always expire on the last business day of the month

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** American options provide MORE flexibility (exercise anytime) compared to European options (exercise only at expiry). More flexibility = more value = higher premium.
**The Trap:** The premium difference is due to exercise flexibility, not exchange size or margin requirements.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Since India uses only European options, this is tested as a theoretical concept.
**Key Takeaway:** More flexibility = higher value. American option premium >= European option premium (all else equal).
